author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
kṛṣṇavārtā vinā āna, ‘asadvārtā’ bali’ jāna,
sei veśyā ati bhayaṅkarī
śrī⋅kṛṣṇaviṣayā mati, jīvera durlabha ati,
sei veśyā mati laya hari’

śuna, mana, bali he tomāya
‘mukti’⋅nāme śārdūlinī, tā’ra kathā jadi śuni,
sarvātmasampatti gili’ khāya

tadubhaya tyāga kara, muktikathā parihara,
lakṣmīpati⋅rati rākha dūre
se⋅rati prabala ha’le, paravyome deya phele,
nāhi deya vāsa vrajapure

vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅rati, amūlya⋅dhanada ati,
tā’i tumi bhaja ciradina
rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pāya, sei rati prārthanāya,
e bhaktivinoda dīna hīna
en_translation:
Without topics concerning Lord Kṛṣṇa, know all other talk to be "unreal talk." Such talk is like a very dreadful prostitute. Devotion concerning Lord Kṛṣṇa is extremely rare for the soul, and that prostitute steals away that devotion.

Listen, O my mind, I tell you: if one listens to the tigress named "liberation," she swallows all his spiritual wealth.

Abandon both sense gratification and liberation, and give up talk of impersonal liberation. Keep devotion for Lord Nārāyaṇa far away. If that devotion becomes strong, it throws one into the spiritual sky of Vaikuṇṭha, but does not grant residence in Vraja.

Devotion for Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja is an extremely priceless bestower of wealth. Therefore, you should always worship that. At the feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī, praying for that devotion, this Bhaktivinoda is poor and humble.
